title: Executive Order 12088—Federal Compliance With Pollution Control Standards

flags.justification: A multi-layered, distributed compliance system: individual agency heads bear compliance responsibility, EPA provides technical oversight, and OMB serves as an escalation point for unresolved conflicts.

Sec. 1-103 defines 'applicable pollution control standards' as 'the same substantive, procedural, and other requirements that would apply to a private person' — a clear, objective standard with no special government carve-out, combined with a detailed annual planning and reporting requirement and a clear conflict-escalation chain (agency to EPA to OMB).

Multiple pollution-control statutes (TSCA, Clean Water Act, Safe Drinking Water Act, Clean Air Act, and others) are covered, but all coherently serve one purpose: federal facility compliance with environmental law.

The 'same standard as a private person' definition (Sec. 1-103) is objective, tying federal compliance to whatever standard already applies externally, with no separately-defined vague federal standard.
The order specifically prevents the federal government from operating under weaker standards than private parties, closing off a potential loophole rather than creating one.
A standing compliance framework for an ongoing government function, evergreen by nature.

Sec. 1-604 explicitly states the conflict-resolution procedures are 'in addition to, not in lieu of, other procedures, including sanctions, for the enforcement of applicable pollution control standards' — explicitly preserving rather than substituting for normal enforcement.

Sec. 1-601 requires a violating agency to obtain the notifying agency's approval of its compliance plan — real, substantial approval authority held by state, local, or interstate agencies over federal facility compliance, not merely a consultation requirement.

This establishes a comprehensive, durable federal-facility environmental-compliance framework covering essentially every major pollution-control statute — a significant, lasting institutional structure.
EPA, OMB, and individual agency roles are clearly delineated with no competing mandate.

Sec. 1-701 requires exemptions to rest on a specific presidential determination tied to narrow statutory grounds (national security or paramount U.S. interest), with mandatory EPA input (1-703) and a sixty-day OMB response deadline (1-704) — a narrow, high-threshold, presidentially-gated process, not a broad or easy carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/22 = 4.5%. One of the most exemplary orders coded in this project to date: an objective same-standard-as-private-parties compliance rule, explicit non-substitution for normal enforcement, real external (state/local) approval authority over federal compliance plans, and a narrow, presidentially-gated exemption process requiring EPA sign-off. A strong candidate example of well-designed governance architecture.
